member.office_address: 2303 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-1404

form_analysis.form_header_text: Office of Congressman Jim Baird - FY27 Appropriations Support Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: The House Appropriations Committee sets rigid deadlines for offices to submit requests, and limits the number of requests each member can make. In order to ensure we can adequately review and submit requests, this form should be submitted as soon as possible. UPDATE: The Appropriations Committee has announced the deadlines for members requests for the FY2027 appropriations process. In order to meet the Committee's deadline, any proposals for Programmatic or Language requests to Congressman Baird must be received by: Friday, March 6, 2026, at 6:00 p.m. (Ag, CJS, FSGG, Legislative, MilCon-VA, State/Foreign Ops); Thursday, March 12, 2026, at 6:00 p.m. (Defense, Energy & Water, Homeland, Interior, Labor-HHS, THUD). Community Project Funding request deadlines: Monday, March 16, 2026, at 6:00 p.m. (CJS, MilCon-VA); Monday, March 23, 2026, at 6:00 p.m. (Ag); Monday, April 6, 2026, at 6:00 p.m. (Energy & Water, Homeland, Interior, Labor-HHS, THUD). Please provide as much information as you can so we can fully evaluate your submission. Requests received after submission deadline may be reviewed on a case by case basis. Incomplete forms may not receive consideration. Please fill out ONE form per request. You may submit multiple requests, but please prioritize your submissions. If you have questions, please email BairdAppropration@mail.house.gov.

sections.description: Basic information about the organization and the point of contact for the request.

fields.label: Title of Your Request (short)
fields.draft_value: NASA Science Mission Directorate Funding
fields.rationale: Uses the standard account name from the reference values for a programmatic request.

fields.label: Appropriations Request Type:
options: Programmatic (Funding for Existing Program)
Language (Proposal for a New Program or Changes to an Existing Program)
Community Project Funding Request (Funding for a Specific Project within the District)
fields.draft_value: Programmatic (Funding for Existing Program)
fields.rationale: The request is for the NASA Science Mission Directorate, which is an existing program within NASA, as specified in the reference values.

sections.description: Specific details for programmatic funding requests.

fields.label: Programmatic Request Title
fields.draft_value: NASA Science Mission Directorate
fields.rationale: Directly matches the short title provided in the reference context.

fields.label: Programmatic Request Description
fields.draft_value:
* $9 billion for the NASA Science Mission Directorate (SMD) to support competitive, peer-reviewed missions across Earth Science, Planetary Science, Astrophysics, Heliophysics, and Biological and Physical Sciences.
* This funding levels out the significant disruptions caused in FY 2026 by restoring investments to levels consistent with bipartisan congressional intent and Decadal Survey recommendations.
* Provides stable resources for critical missions that maintain U.S. dominance in space exploration and scientific discovery.
fields.rationale: This value is based on the provided $9 billion funding ask for NASA SMD, framed as a programmatic request to restore stability after FY2026.

fields.label: How does this project or request relate to Indiana's 4th District?
fields.draft_value:
* Direct local economic impact through $15,391,946.83 in NASA Science obligations within Indiana's 4th District from FY2023 to FY2025.
* Purdue University in West Lafayette is a top recipient, with 12 grants in FY2023-FY2025 totaling over $14.1 million to advance national space priorities.
* Specific district-led research includes a $4 million award for Moon-Mars situational awareness and critical projects developing power solutions for crewed Mars missions.
* Supports high-tech jobs and the STEM workforce pipeline at Purdue University, directly contributing to Indiana's leadership in aerospace innovation.
fields.rationale: Cites specific NASA spending data for IN-4, highlighting Purdue University's $15.3M in obligations and specific active grant values/descriptions.

fields.label: Are you submitting multiple programmatic requests?
options: Yes
No
Maybe
fields.rationale: This information is specific to the submitter's full slate of requests, which is not provided in the context.

fields.label: Please identify how many requests you are submitting, and how this request ranks in relation to your other requests (i.e. This request is priority 1 of 3)
fields.rationale: This is a user-specific prioritization question.

fields.label: Please list other offices (if any) receiving this request?
fields.draft_value:
* Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request.
* Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: If there is another office leading a Member letter on this request, please provide the Office and Staff POC (name and email)
fields.draft_value: Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA)
fields.rationale: Identifies the lead offices for the existing House member letter mentioned in the reference values.

fields.label: Which Appropriations Subcommittee has jurisdiction over this funding request?
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Defense
Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies
Financial Services and General Government
Homeland Security
Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies
Labor Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Legislative Branch
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs
Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA funding falls under the jurisdiction of the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Subcommittee.

fields.label: Program (if known)
fields.draft_value: Science Mission Directorate
fields.rationale: Identifies the specific program/directorate within NASA as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Department / Agency / Bureau
fields.draft_value: NASA
fields.rationale: Authoritative agency name.

fields.label: Account (if known)
fields.draft_value: Science
fields.rationale: Standard NASA appropriations account name.

fields.label: How does your request fit into the President's Budget Request (PBR)?
options: Increase in the PBR (i.e. Increase the $10M requested by the President for <<Programmatic Request>> to $11M)
Clarification / of the PBR (i.e. Of the $10M requested in the PBR, $1M shall be spent on <<Programmatic Request>>)
Addition to the PBR (i.e. Provide $1M to fund <<Programmatic Request>>)
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request has not been released yet, making it impossible to determine if the $9B request represents an increase, clarification, or addition to the PBR.

fields.label: Total Amount Requested for Program
fields.rationale: Matches the specific funding ask amount from the reference values.

fields.label: If you are requesting an increase in funding to what is requested in the PBR, what do you propose reducing in the PBR to pay for the requested increase?
fields.rationale: Specific offset suggestions are outside the scope of provided context and require the submitter's policy preference.

fields.label: Provide your proposed Programming Funding justification language
fields.draft_value:
* U.S. Space Leadership: Ensures the United States maintains its competitive edge over China's rapidly expanding space science program, which is already targeting Mars and the Moon's far side.
* Economic Multiplier: Every dollar invested in NASA Science returns roughly $3 to the economy, supporting research across 66 grants in FY2023-FY2025 and Indiana's high-tech manufacturing base.
* Workforce Stability: Sustained funding prevents the loss of specialized talent following the workforce disruptions and layoffs experienced during the FY 2026 budget cycle.
* Scientific Rigor: Follows nonpartisan, peer-reviewed roadmaps from the National Academies' Decadal Surveys to address the most significant questions in space exploration.
fields.rationale: Uses advocacy talking points including the 3:1 economic return, competition with China, and the need for workforce stability following FY2026 disruptions.

fields.label: Consider the amount provided for this program in the FY26 Consolidated Appropriations Act.
options: Increase to the amount appropriated in FY26?
Decrease to the amount appropriated in FY26?
The same as was appropriated in FY26?
fields.draft_value: Increase to the amount appropriated in FY26?
fields.rationale: The FY2026 enacted amount was $7.250 billion. The current request is $9.000 billion, which is a $1.750 billion increase.

fields.label: Has there been a Dear Colleague or other correspondence circulated with this request? Please identify the sponsoring office(s)
fields.draft_value: Yes, a House member letter led by Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA).
fields.rationale: References the existing letter context provided.

fields.label: Is there anything else you would like to add to this request?
fields.draft_value:
* Space science is a fundamental public responsibility codified in Title 51 of the U.S. Code that advances national security and economic competitiveness.
* Public polling consistently shows space science is among NASA's most popular and valued activities across both parties.
* Robust funding for SMD is essential for planetary defense and weather forecasting capabilities that protect American lives and property.
fields.rationale: Draws on the broader value of space science as a public responsibility and its high public polling support as mentioned in talking points.

sections.description: Specific details for Community Project Funding (CPF) requests.

fields.label: Community Project Funding Request Title
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic request, not a Community Project Funding (earmark) request; this section does not apply.

fields.label: Community Project Funding Request Description
fields.rationale: This request is categorized as a Programmatic request, not a Community Project Funding request.

fields.label: How does this request benefit the people of Indiana's 4th District?

fields.label: How does your request fit into the President's Budget Request (PBR)?
options: Increase in the PBR (i.e. Increase the $10M requested by the President for <<Community Funding Request>> to $11M)
Clarification / of the PBR (i.e. Of the $10M requested in the PBR, $1M shall be spent on <<Community Funding Request>>)
Addition to the PBR (i.e. Provide $1M to fund <<Community Funding Request>>)
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request amount is unknown; furthermore, this field appears to be in a section for 'Community Funding Requests' which does not apply to this programmatic request.

fields.label: Total Amount Requested for Program
fields.rationale: This field is within the Community Project Funding section which does not apply.

fields.label: Consider the amount of federal funds provided for this program in FY26.
options: Increase to the amount appropriated in FY26?
Decrease to the amount appropriated in FY26?
The same as was appropriated in FY26?
A new request that did not receive any federal funding in FY26?
fields.draft_value: Increase to the amount appropriated in FY26?
fields.rationale: The $9B request for SMD is an increase over the FY2026 enacted level of $7.250B.

fields.label: Has there been a Dear Colleague or other correspondence circulated with this request? Please identify the sponsoring office(s)
fields.rationale: This field is within the Community Project Funding section which does not apply.

fields.label: Is there anything else you would like to add to this request?
fields.rationale: This field belongs to the Community Project Funding section which does not apply to this programmatic request.

fields.rationale: The request context specifies that no bill or report language is being requested (N/A).

fields.label: Language Type
options: Bill
Report
fields.rationale: This request is programmatic; reference values indicate N/A for bill and report language.

fields.label: Is there related language in the President's Budget request?

fields.label: Was similar language included in the FY26 Appropriations Bill or Report?
fields.rationale: The request context specifies that no bill or report language is being requested (N/A).

fields.label: Has there been a Dear Colleague or other correspondence circulated regarding this request? Please identify the sponsoring office(s).
fields.draft_value:
* Yes, a House programmatic support letter has been circulated to support NASA Science Mission Directorate funding levels.
* The letter is led by Representatives Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA).
fields.rationale: The reference values explicitly identify the House letter led by Reps. Bacon and Chu.

form_analysis.deadline_text: Programmatic/Language: March 6 & 12, 2026; Community Projects: March 16, March 23 & April 6, 2026

top_grants.description: Execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress.   Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond.   Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines   develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Advancing Moon-Mars science and critical space situational awareness research.

top_grants.description: 23-picasso23-0011   fine-scale pixel element mapping for Mars  the moon  and other bodies with miniaturized libs
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Producing fine-scale surface maps for Mars and the Moon.

top_grants.description: As space missions increase in scope  size  complexity  and duration  so do both power and heat dissipation demands. This is particularly the case for future manned missions to Mars. Paramount to the success of these missions is ability to reduce siz
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing compact power solutions for crewed Mars missions.

top_grants.description: e014042- role of linked hydrological  permafrost  ground ice  and land cover changes in regional carbon balance across boreal and arctic landscapes
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Modeling regional carbon balance changes in Arctic permafrost.

top_grants.description: Wildfire burn severity has been rarely factored into regional carbon budget quantification due to lacking the spatially and temporally explicit data. Here we propose to use a process-based model driven with satellite-derived burn severity data to
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Quantifying regional carbon budgets using satellite wildfire data.

top_grants.description: Significantly  this approach will enable time and resource efficient analysis and predictive capacities that will allow for the implementation of active strategies for habs management in real time as well as across field seasons.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing predictive tools for real-time habitat management strategies.
